analysis.summary: President Trump set an 8 p.m. ET Tuesday deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and reach a peace deal, threatening devastating military strikes on civilian infrastructure including power plants and bridges. Trump warned that 'a whole civilization will die tonight' if no deal was reached, while the U.S. military struck Iran's Kharg Island ahead of the deadline. The ultimatum triggered market turbulence, diplomatic responses from Pakistan and China, a failed UN resolution vetoed by Russia and China, and sharp domestic political backlash including Democratic calls to invoke the 25th Amendment.
